name: Smart Building IoT Botnet & Pivot
description: Compromise BACnet/KNX/Zigbee-connected building automation IoT devices (HVAC controllers, lighting, access control, fire panels) to build a botnet, exfiltrate data, or pivot into the corporate network.
root_objective: Compromise smart building IoT devices to build a botnet or pivot into the corporate network

nodes.title: Weaponize Smart Building IoT for Botnet or Network Pivot
nodes.description: Compromise building management system (BMS) controllers and IoT sensors connected via BACnet, KNX, Zigbee, or EnOcean to establish a persistent botnet, pivot into the corporate LAN, or cause physical disruption (HVAC, lighting, access control).

nodes.title: Compromise BMS Head-End Server
nodes.description: Attack the Building Management System head-end server (Honeywell Tridium Niagara, Schneider EcoStruxure, Siemens Desigo) to gain centralised control over all building subsystems.

nodes.title: Mass Compromise IoT Field Devices
nodes.description: Directly infect individual IoT controllers, sensors, and actuators to build a distributed botnet across the building's BACnet/IP or KNX network.

nodes.title: Exploit Niagara/Tridium JACE Controller CVEs
nodes.description: Target known vulnerabilities in Tridium Niagara JACE controllers (e.g., CVE-2012-4701, CVE-2017-16744) to gain admin access to the BMS head-end.

nodes.title: Credential Stuffing on BMS Web Portal
nodes.description: Access the BMS web management portal using default or leaked credentials. Many building operators never change factory defaults.

nodes.title: Pivot from BMS to Corporate Network
nodes.description: Use the BMS server's network interfaces (often connected to both building OT and corporate IT VLANs) to pivot into the enterprise active directory environment.

nodes.title: Scan and Exploit BACnet/IP Devices
nodes.description: Perform BACnet service discovery (port 47808) and exploit unauthenticated BACnet write-property commands to take control of HVAC controllers, VAV boxes, and lighting panels.

nodes.title: Compromise Zigbee/Z-Wave Mesh Network
nodes.description: Sniff and replay Zigbee/Z-Wave traffic to learn network keys, then inject commands to sensors and actuators across the building's wireless mesh.

nodes.title: Install Crypto-Miner or DDoS Agent on IoT Devices
nodes.description: Deploy lightweight malware on compromised building IoT devices (ARM/MIPS Linux) to participate in DDoS botnets or cryptocurrency mining operations.

nodes.title: BACnet Protocol Has No Authentication
nodes.description: BACnet/IP has no built-in authentication or encryption. Any device on the BACnet segment can read or write properties on any other device.

nodes.title: IoT Devices Run Outdated Embedded Linux
nodes.description: Building IoT controllers run old embedded Linux kernels (3.x/4.x) with known privilege escalation vulnerabilities and no automatic update mechanism.

nodes.title: BMS Server Dual-Homed on IT and OT VLANs
nodes.description: The BMS head-end server has network interfaces on both the building automation VLAN and the corporate IT VLAN, providing a pivot point into the enterprise network.
